test_cases.description: Verifies that main() successfully fetches all user records when JSONPlaceholder API is available and functioning.
test_cases.name: test_successful_fetch_of_all_users

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is reachable and returns a valid list of user records.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called. The HTTP request is sent to the API. All user records from the response are logged (summaries), and the count is logged. No errors are raised.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Checks that main() handles the scenario where the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable and does not proceed with fetching or logging user summaries.
test_cases.name: test_api_unreachable

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is unreachable (e.g., network error, DNS failure, API down).
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called and fails. An error is logged indicating the failure, and no user summaries or counts are logged. The application does not crash.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Verifies that main() correctly handles the case where the API returns an empty list of users.
test_cases.name: test_api_returns_empty_user_list

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is reachable and returns an empty list.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called and returns an empty list. The user count logged is 0, and no user summaries are logged.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Checks that main() handles invalid JSON response from the API gracefully.
test_cases.name: test_api_returns_invalid_json

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is reachable but responds with invalid (malformed) JSON.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called and raises a parsing exception. An error message is logged, and no user summaries or counts are logged. The application does not crash.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Ensures main() can log user summaries even if some user records from the API are missing optional fields, as long as required ones are present.
test_cases.name: test_api_returns_partial_user_data

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is reachable and returns user records with some missing optional fields (e.g., missing phone or website).
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called and returns the partial data. The user count is logged, and summaries for each user (with available fields) are logged. No errors occur.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Checks that main() handles the case where the API returns a valid JSON response that is not a list (e.g., an object or error message).
test_cases.name: test_api_returns_non_list_response

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is reachable and returns a JSON object instead of a list.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called, detects an unexpected response type, and logs an error. No user summaries or counts are logged. The application does not crash.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Verifies that main() does not execute its code when src/main.py is imported as a module rather than run directly.
test_cases.name: test_run_as_imported_module

test_setup.given: The src/main.py file is imported as a module in another script.
test_setup.then: No logging is initialized, no user data fetch occurs, and no HTTP requests are made. The application remains idle until main() is explicitly called.
test_setup.when: The main() function is not called directly.

test_cases.description: Ensures that logging is properly initialized at the start of main(), and all subsequent logs are recorded at the correct log levels.
test_cases.name: test_logging_initialization

test_setup.given: The script is run directly, and logging configuration is not pre-set.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized at the start. All important events (start, user fetch, user count, user summaries, errors) are logged according to their severity.

test_cases.description: Checks that main() handles the case where the API is slow to respond and an HTTP timeout occurs.
test_cases.name: test_api_slow_response_timeout

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is extremely slow and triggers an HTTP timeout.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called and a timeout exception is raised. An error message is logged, and no user summaries or counts are logged. The application does not crash.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Verifies main() processes and logs all user records even if the API returns duplicate user entries.
test_cases.name: test_api_returns_duplicate_users

test_setup.given: The JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint is reachable and returns a list with duplicate user records.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized. A message indicating the start of user data fetch is logged. UserService is created. get_all_users() is called and returns the list with duplicates. The user count reflects the total number of records received, and summaries for all users (including duplicates) are logged. No errors occur.
test_setup.when: The main() function is called directly.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() retrieves and returns all user records when the JSONPlaceholder API is available.
test_cases.name: test_all_users_success

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns a valid list of 10 user objects at /users.
test_setup.then: A list of 10 User domain models is returned, matching the API response, with no errors.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() handles the scenario where the API returns an empty user list.
test_cases.name: test_empty_user_list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns an empty list at /users.
test_setup.then: An empty list is returned, with no errors.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() handles the scenario when the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable.
test_cases.name: test_api_unreachable

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is down or network connection fails.
test_setup.then: A handled error is reported (e.g., exception raised or error returned), and no user data is provided.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() handles HTTP error responses from the API.
test_cases.name: test_api_http_error

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API responds with an HTTP error status (e.g., 500, 404) to the /users request.
test_setup.then: A handled error is reported (e.g., exception raised or error returned), and no user data is provided.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() handles the scenario where the API returns invalid JSON.
test_cases.name: test_api_invalid_json

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns malformed or non-JSON response at /users.
test_setup.then: A handled error is reported (e.g., exception raised or error returned), and no user data is provided.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() can handle user records with missing or null fields.
test_cases.name: test_user_records_with_missing_fields

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns a list of users, some of which have missing or null properties.
test_setup.then: Returned list contains User domain models reflecting the missing/null fields as per API response, with no errors.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() can handle a very large number of user records returned by the API.
test_cases.name: test_large_user_list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns a very large list (e.g., 10,000 users) at /users.
test_setup.then: A list of 10,000 User domain models is returned, matching the API response, with no errors or performance issues.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() handles the scenario where the API returns a valid JSON object that is not a list.
test_cases.name: test_api_returns_non_list_object

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns a JSON object (not a list) at /users.
test_setup.then: A handled error is reported (e.g., exception raised or error returned), and no user data is provided.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() returns duplicate records if present in API response, without filtering.
test_cases.name: test_duplicate_user_records

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns a list of users with duplicate entries.
test_setup.then: Returned list contains all duplicates exactly as received, with no filtering or deduplication.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users() correctly returns user records with special or non-ASCII characters in their fields.
test_cases.name: test_special_characters_in_user_data

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns user records with special characters in names, emails, etc.
test_setup.then: Returned User domain models contain the special characters as provided, with no data corruption.

test_cases.description: Test that all user records are fetched successfully when the JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns valid data.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_success

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable, /users endpoint returns HTTP 200 with a valid list of user records (10 users).
test_setup.then: A list of 10 User domain models is returned, each corresponding to a user record from the API. No errors are raised.

test_cases.description: Test that an error is raised when the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable (e.g., network failure or host not found).
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_api_unreachable

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable due to network failure or incorrect domain.
test_setup.then: A connection or request exception is raised, and no user data is returned.

test_cases.description: Test that an error is raised when the /users endpoint returns an HTTP error status (e.g., 500 Internal Server Error).
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_http_error

test_setup.given: /users endpoint returns HTTP 500 error.
test_setup.then: An HTTP error is raised, and no user data is returned.

test_cases.description: Test that an empty list is returned when the API returns an empty list of users.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_empty_list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable, /users endpoint returns HTTP 200 with an empty list [].
test_setup.then: An empty list is returned. No errors are raised.

test_cases.description: Test that a JSON parsing error is raised if the API returns malformed JSON.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_malformed_json

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable, /users endpoint returns malformed JSON.
test_setup.then: A JSONDecodeError (or equivalent parsing error) is raised. No user data is returned.

test_cases.description: Test that incomplete user objects are still parsed as User domain models if required fields are present, or an error is raised for missing mandatory fields.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_partial_user_objects

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns a list with some user objects missing optional fields, but all mandatory fields exist.
test_setup.then: Users with all mandatory fields are parsed into User domain models. If mandatory fields are missing, an error is raised for those entries.

test_cases.description: Test that an error is raised if the API response is valid JSON but not a list (e.g., a JSON object or string).
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_non_list_json

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns HTTP 200 with a JSON object (not a list).
test_setup.then: A type error or custom exception is raised indicating the expected list structure was not found.

test_cases.description: Test that a large number of user records are handled correctly by the function.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_large_number_of_users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns HTTP 200 with a list of 1000 valid user objects.
test_setup.then: A list of 1000 User domain models is returned. No errors or performance issues occur.

test_cases.description: Test that user objects with extra, unexpected fields do not affect parsing into User domain models.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_unexpected_fields

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns user objects with additional fields not defined in the User domain model.
test_setup.then: User domain models are parsed using only the expected fields. Extra fields are ignored.

test_cases.description: Test that an error is raised if a user entry in the returned list is not a dictionary (e.g., a string or number).
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_invalid_user_object_type

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns a list containing an element that is not a dictionary.
test_setup.then: A type error or custom exception is raised for invalid user object type.
